question.message: Would it be stupid for me to pause my 401k contributions for a few months to build up cash reserves?

trials.answer: Pausing your 401(k) contributions is a common trade-off between building cash for today and investing for tomorrow. While it would help you reach your $50,000 emergency fund goal faster, the biggest risk is missing out on a potential employer match and failing to max out your contributions for the year. A less drastic option would be to redirect other savings, like taxable brokerage contributions, toward your cash goal first.

This is a classic trade-off between building short-term stability and achieving long-term growth. Pausing your 401(k) contributions would quickly build your cash reserves from their current $0 balance, but it would come at the cost of your 20% savings rate goal and any employer match you might receive.
